mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 3-month average growth is positive, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a certain threshold signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, thus driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation between unemployment spikes and recessions
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rate and low inflation suggests an accommodative monetary environment, reducing borrowing costs and increasing real disposable income, which supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates and inflation support equity valuations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 2%, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts during an economic expansion indicate robust investment and consumer confidence, which typically precedes positive short-term market sentiment and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month average is above 600k units during an expansion, but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in GDP growth signals weakening economic activity, which negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to a decline in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a key driver of corporate profits
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1% annualized, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy labor market and strong consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average change is above 100k, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, which translates to higher corporate output and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month change is greater than 0.5%, but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rate and strong retail sales indicates that accommodative monetary policy is effectively stimulating consumer demand, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and RSAFS 3-month average growth is above 0.3%, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.
